commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5910c0b79682be46a526e613f81ec869ee24c1c8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory chunking to avoid UTF-16 surrogate-pair boundary splits, adds embedding-side unpaired-surrogate sanitization, and removes the memory host SDK internal test file.

PR surface: Source +61, Tests -430. Total -369 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The underlying bug is source-reproducible from current main because the coarse splitter can cut at `maxChars` without checking surrogate boundaries, and the linked issue reports live memory index failures from that exact class of split.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action is needed because the PR is dirty, has blocking review findings, and lacks sufficient current real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(memory): prevent indexing stalls from emoji surrogate pair splits
target.body:
Fixes #65782

Prevent memory indexing stalls caused by splitting UTF-16 surrogate pairs (emojis, supplementary-plane characters) during chunking.

## Changes

- **chunkMarkdown**: outer loop now guards against cutting inside surrogate pairs (mirrors inner loop logic)
- **stripUnpairedSurrogates**: defense-in-depth sanitization before sending to embedding APIs
- **Tests**: regression test for emoji boundary case + comprehensive unit tests for sanitization

## Testing

- All new tests pass
- Verified exact boundary case from issue: 39 ASCII + 🌸
- No lone surrogates appear in chunked output
- Embedding calls receive sanitized text

## Impact

This fixes a critical production bug where indexing would permanently stall after encountering an emoji split across chunk boundaries. The fix is minimal, backward-compatible, and includes defense-in-depth at the embedding layer.
target.author: zehanxu-doncic
